name: Pawn & Sell / Hillside
description_short: Pawn & Sell operates three locations across Queens and Flushing, NY, offering collateral-based loans, jewelry buying, and pawning services with extended evening hours.
description_long:
Pawn & Sell is a multi-location pawn shop operator serving the Queens and greater New York area with three storefront locations in Queens, Hollis, and Flushing. The company specializes in jewelry transactions, including buying unwanted jewelry and providing pawn loans secured by personal items. The business has been established enough to maintain a professional web presence and multiple operational locations across different neighborhoods.

The company offers traditional pawn shop services: accepting items as collateral for short-term loans, purchasing jewelry outright, and facilitating redemptions of pawned goods. They accept walk-in customers during business hours and provide phone-based inquiry options. Their service model is designed for customers needing immediate liquidity without credit checks or lengthy approval processes.

Pawn & Sell distinguishes itself through extended operating hours at its flagship Queens location (open until midnight Thursday-Saturday) and strategic location placement in high-traffic NYC neighborhoods. They maintain multiple contact channels including phone lines, email, and a functional website with location maps. The Hillside Avenue location in Hollis operates more limited hours, suggesting tailored service to different neighborhood demographics.

Pawn shops provide legitimate short-term financial access but carry inherent trade-offs: borrowers risk losing collateral if loans aren't repaid, jewelry purchases occur at below-market rates, and the implied APR on pawn loans is often substantially higher than traditional lending. This service is best suited for customers with immediate cash needs, no credit history, or those unwilling to undergo formal loan underwriting. Customers should fully understand redemption deadlines and valuation terms before transacting.
pros: Three convenient locations across Queens (Conduit Ave), Hollis (Hillside Ave), and Flushing (Northern Blvd) for neighborhood accessibility
Extended hours at Queens flagship location: open until midnight Thursday-Saturday for customer convenience
No credit check or financial underwriting required—instant access to cash for those unable to qualify for traditional loans
Direct jewelry buying service allows immediate payment for unwanted items without consignment delays
Multiple contact methods: phone (three separate lines), email, and web-based inquiry system
Transparent pricing and clear redemption windows posted at each location
Established business with professional website and location mapping for customer navigation
cons: Collateral-at-risk model means failure to repay results in permanent loss of personal items, with no credit-building benefit
Implied APR on pawn loans typically ranges 240-600% annualized when accounting for short redemption windows and principal amounts
Jewelry valuations are inherently one-sided: shops purchase at significant discounts to resale value (typically 40-60% below market)
Limited hours at non-flagship locations (Hollis 10am-8pm weekdays only, Flushing closed weekends) reduce accessibility
No alternative payment plans or negotiation paths once redemption deadline passes—items are forfeited and sold
best_for: Individuals with immediate emergency cash needs ($200-$3,000) and collateral but no access to traditional credit
People with no established credit history or past credit damage who cannot qualify for personal loans
Customers holding unwanted jewelry or gold who prefer instant payment over consignment or online selling
Borrowers needing small short-term loans (2-4 months) who can reliably retrieve collateral before redemption deadlines
diagnosis: Pawn & Sell is appropriate for consumers facing genuine financial emergencies who own collateral and cannot access traditional credit—notably unbanked or credit-impaired individuals. The critical caveat is understanding that pawning is expensive short-term financing (often 300%+ annualized) and carries the risk of permanent loss of personal items; customers should exhaust alternatives (credit cards, family, credit unions, employer advances) before pawning irreplaceable possessions.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal items, jewelry, electronics, and collectibles
Jewelry buying—direct cash purchases for gold, silver, diamonds, and watches
Walk-in pawn transactions with same-day funding
Extended evening and weekend hours for accessibility
Web-based location finder and hours verification
